metadata.archetype_justification: This is a Windows desktop application with no persistent data store, no API surface, no database connections, and no downstream service calls. All operations are local computation (screenshot capture, image processing, annotation). Classified as stateless-utility.

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No cloud compute — application is a Windows desktop binary with no managed services
top_gaps.impact: Cannot scale, patch, or manage compute through cloud services

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: No IaC of any kind — all infrastructure managed manually
top_gaps.impact: No reproducibility, no environment consistency, no DR capability

top_gaps.title: Integration Testing
top_gaps.gap: No automated tests of any kind exist in the repository
top_gaps.impact: No regression safety net for any code changes

top_gaps.title: Compute Hardening and Patching
top_gaps.gap: No vulnerability scanning or patching strategy for dependencies
top_gaps.impact: Known vulnerabilities in dependencies may reach production undetected

top_gaps.title: Application Security Pipeline
top_gaps.gap: No SAST, DAST, or dependency scanning in CI/CD pipeline
top_gaps.impact: Security vulnerabilities in code and dependencies are never automatically detected

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 = 2). GitHub Actions workflows are configured with build and deploy stages.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that triggers deployments, checks build status, manages release notes, and coordinates the release process.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: GitHub Actions API access is already available via GITHUB_TOKEN.

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists in repository. README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, copilot-instructions.md, docs/ directory with release management and changelog documentation.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A knowledge agent that answers developer questions about the Greenshot build process, architecture, contribution guidelines, and release procedures.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Index existing documentation files. The .github/copilot-instructions.md file is particularly comprehensive.

approaches.name: Strangler Fig (Parallel Track)
approaches.description: Extract cloud upload plugins into independent backend services while keeping the desktop app as the primary interface.

approaches.name: Conditional / Adaptive
approaches.description: Keep the desktop app as-is but add a cloud backend for new features (cross-device sync, web-based annotation).

approaches.name: Big-Bang Rewrite
approaches.description: Rewrite Greenshot as a cloud-native web application replacing the desktop client entirely.

findings.title: No managed compute — desktop application with no cloud services
findings.description: Greenshot is a Windows desktop application compiled as a WinExe binary targeting .NET Framework 4.8.0. There is no cloud compute of any kind — no EC2, no ECS/EKS, no Lambda, no Fargate.
findings.gap: No managed compute — the application has no server-side component. All processing occurs on the user's local machine.
findings.recommendation: If cloud features are desired (e.g., cross-device screenshot sync, cloud-based annotation), consider building backend services using Lambda or ECS with EKS as preferred by organizational preferences.

findings.title: No network security infrastructure
findings.description: No VPC, security groups, subnets, or network segmentation exists. This is a desktop application with no cloud infrastructure.
findings.gap: No cloud network security infrastructure exists. The application communicates directly from user machines to external cloud services over the public internet.
findings.recommendation: If backend services are built in the future, deploy them in a VPC with private subnets, least-privilege security groups, and VPC endpoints for AWS service access.

findings.title: No API entry point
findings.description: No API Gateway, ALB, CloudFront, or any managed entry point exists. The application does not expose APIs — it is a desktop tool that consumes external APIs.
findings.gap: No API entry point exists because no APIs are exposed.
findings.recommendation: If cloud backend services are built, use API Gateway with throttling and authentication as the entry point.

findings.title: No auto-scaling
findings.description: No auto-scaling exists. There are no scalable cloud resources — no ASGs, no ECS services, no Lambda functions.
findings.gap: No cloud resources exist that could benefit from auto-scaling.
findings.recommendation: If cloud services are built in the future, configure auto-scaling on all compute and data resources from day one.

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No infrastructure as code of any kind exists in this repository. No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm, or other IaC tools are present.
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage — all infrastructure (GitHub Pages, CloudFlare cache, Chocolatey publishing) is configured manually outside the repository.
findings.recommendation: Define any cloud infrastructure as code. If migrating to AWS, use CDK or Terraform to define all resources.

findings.title: Partial CI/CD automation — build automated but no testing or security stages
findings.description: GitHub Actions provides automated build and release artifact creation but lacks test stages, security scanning, and staged deployment.
findings.gap: Build is automated but there is no test stage, no security scanning stage, and no staged deployment.
findings.recommendation: Add test stages to the pipeline. Integrate Dependabot for NuGet vulnerability scanning. Add a SAST tool as a required pipeline step before artifact creation.

findings.title: Legacy .NET Framework 4.8 — compound framework regression
findings.description: The application is written in C# targeting .NET Framework 4.8.0 (legacy, maintenance mode). This is NOT modern .NET (6/7/8/9/10).
findings.gap: .NET Framework 4.8 lacks modern .NET features: cross-platform support, performance improvements, container-optimized builds, native AOT. The broader cloud-native .NET ecosystem targets modern .NET only.
findings.recommendation: Migrate from .NET Framework 4.8 to modern .NET (8 or 9 LTS). WinForms and WPF are supported on modern .NET for Windows-only applications.

findings.title: Monolith with identifiable plugin boundaries
findings.description: Greenshot is a monolithic desktop application with 12 projects including 7 plugins. Plugins are compiled as separate DLLs but loaded at runtime into the same process.
findings.gap: Single deployable unit. All components share the same process, memory space, and lifecycle. Cloud upload functionality is tightly coupled to the desktop app's runtime.
findings.recommendation: The plugin architecture provides natural decomposition boundaries. Each cloud upload plugin could be extracted into an independent backend service if cloud-native features are planned.

findings.title: No API versioning — no APIs exposed
findings.description: No API versioning exists because no APIs are exposed. Greenshot is a consumer of external APIs but does not expose any API surface of its own.
findings.gap: No API versioning strategy. If cloud backend services are built, a versioning strategy needs to be established.
findings.recommendation: If backend services are introduced, adopt URL-path versioning (e.g., /v1/) from day one with backward-compatibility guarantees.

findings.title: Hardcoded external service endpoints
findings.description: Service endpoints for cloud integrations are hardcoded in plugin source code. OAuth credentials injected via build-time environment variables.
findings.gap: All external service endpoints are hardcoded in application code. Endpoint changes require code changes and recompilation.
findings.recommendation: Externalize service endpoint URLs into configuration. If cloud backend services are added, adopt environment-variable-based discovery.

findings.title: Screenshots stored on local filesystem only
findings.description: Screenshots and images are stored on the local file system of the user's machine. No cloud object storage integration. No S3, no parsing pipeline.
findings.gap: Unstructured data stored on local file systems with no cloud-based managed storage. No centralized, accessible storage.
findings.recommendation: If cross-device access or centralized screenshot management is desired, integrate S3 for screenshot storage with optional Textract/Rekognition for content extraction.

findings.title: Secrets managed via GitHub Secrets but no rotation
findings.description: OAuth client IDs and secrets are managed through GitHub Secrets and injected at build time via token replacement. No plaintext credentials in source. However, no rotation mechanism exists.
findings.gap: Secrets managed via GitHub Secrets (good) but no rotation mechanism. OAuth client secrets are static and embedded into the compiled binary at build time.
findings.recommendation: Consider using short-lived OAuth tokens with refresh flows. Evaluate whether OAuth public client (PKCE) flows could eliminate the need for client secrets in the distributed binary.

findings.title: No vulnerability scanning or patching strategy
findings.description: No vulnerability scanning or patching strategy exists. No Dependabot, no dotnet audit, no Snyk. NuGet package versions are pinned but not checked for CVEs.
findings.gap: No vulnerability scanning for NuGet dependencies. No automated patching strategy.
findings.recommendation: Enable GitHub Dependabot for NuGet vulnerability scanning. Add dotnet list package --vulnerable to the CI pipeline.

findings.title: No security scanning in CI/CD pipeline
findings.description: No SAST, DAST, or dependency vulnerability scanning is integrated into the CI/CD pipeline. No security validation occurs before artifacts are published.
findings.gap: Complete absence of security scanning. No Dependabot, no SAST tool, no code quality gates.
findings.recommendation: Add GitHub Dependabot for NuGet alerts, GitHub CodeQL for SAST (supports C#), and consider CodeGuru Reviewer if migrating to AWS CI/CD.

findings.title: No distributed tracing
findings.description: No distributed tracing is instrumented. log4net provides local file-based logging only with no structured trace correlation.
findings.gap: No tracing of any kind. No OpenTelemetry, no X-Ray, no trace ID propagation.
findings.recommendation: If cloud backend services are added, instrument with OpenTelemetry from day one. For the current app, consider adding correlation IDs to cloud upload requests.

findings.title: No business metrics
findings.description: No custom business metrics are published. No telemetry, no analytics, no metric collection of any kind.
findings.gap: No business or usage metrics. No understanding of feature usage patterns.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding opt-in telemetry for desktop usage patterns. If cloud services are added, publish business metrics to CloudWatch.

findings.title: No anomaly detection or alerting
findings.description: No anomaly detection or alerting is configured. No CloudWatch alarms, no error rate monitoring, no latency tracking.
findings.gap: No alerting of any kind.
findings.recommendation: If cloud backend services are added, configure CloudWatch anomaly detection on error rates and latency from day one.

findings.title: No automated tests of any kind
findings.description: No automated tests exist in this repository. The copilot-instructions.md explicitly states there are no automated test projects.
findings.gap: Complete absence of automated testing. No regression safety net for code changes.
findings.recommendation: Introduce automated testing: unit tests for core image processing, integration tests for plugin OAuth flows, UI automation tests for critical workflows.

findings.title: No incident response automation or runbooks
findings.description: No incident response automation or runbooks exist. SECURITY.md is minimal with no documented response process.
findings.gap: No incident response procedures documented.
findings.recommendation: Document incident response procedures for security vulnerabilities, broken releases, and OAuth credential rotation.

findings.title: No observability ownership
findings.description: No observability ownership exists. No dashboards, no alarms with owners, no SLO definitions with team attribution.
findings.gap: No observability assets exist to have ownership over.
findings.recommendation: If cloud services are added, establish observability ownership from day one with per-service dashboards and named alarm owners.

findings.title: No resource tagging governance
findings.description: No AWS resources exist to tag. No tagging standard, no tag enforcement, no cost allocation tags.
findings.gap: No resources to tag and no tagging governance.
findings.recommendation: When cloud resources are introduced, establish a mandatory tagging standard enforced via IaC and AWS Config rules.

evaluations.reason: archetype-N/A: stateless-utility, sync is correct design
archetype-N/A: no persistent state to back up
archetype-N/A: no deployed workload requiring HA
archetype-N/A: stateless-utility, sync is correct design
archetype-N/A: no IaC provisioning AWS resources
archetype-N/A: no API surface, no user auth system
archetype-N/A: no user-facing surface for SLOs

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2=2 (monolith), INF-Q1=1 (no managed compute), APP-Q5=1 (no API versioning)

triggering_questions.note: Monolith with identifiable modules but single deployable unit
No cloud compute of any kind

immediate_actions: Identify cloud upload features for extraction
Design backend API for screenshot storage

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=1 but contextual guard blocks: this is a desktop application, not an EC2/VM server workload. No server-side compute exists to containerize.

triggering_questions.note: Score below threshold but contextual guard applies — desktop app, not server workload

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Contextual guard: this is a Windows desktop application, not a server-side workload running on EC2/VM. There is no server-side compute to containerize.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=4 (no stored procedures). No commercial database engines detected.

triggering_questions.note: Score meets threshold — no stored procedures or proprietary SQL

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No commercial database engines detected. No stored procedures or proprietary SQL. DATA-Q4=4 does not meet trigger threshold.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2 Not Evaluated (no persistent data store). No database exists to migrate.

triggering_questions.note: Not Evaluated — no persistent data store exists

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No database exists in this system. INF-Q2 was Not Evaluated due to has_persistent_data_store=false.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4 Not Evaluated. No data processing workloads exist.

triggering_questions.note: Not Evaluated — stateless-utility archetype, no streaming/analytics workloads

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No streaming, ETL, data pipeline, or analytics artifacts found. Desktop application has no data processing responsibilities.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10=1 (no IaC), INF-Q11=2 (partial CI/CD), OPS-Q6=1 (no tests)

triggering_questions.note: Build automated but no testing or security stages

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. The context describes a 'Windows screenshot and annotation tool' with no AI/agent/LLM signal terms.
